evidence: security_headers.py score 50/100; missing HSTS, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, and Referrer-Policy.
impact: Weak browser security posture can reduce trust and expose avoidable UX/security risk.
fix: Add Strict-Transport-Security, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, and Referrer-Policy at the server/CDN level.

finding: AI crawlers are not explicitly managed in robots.txt.
evidence: robots_checker.py found robots.txt HTTP 200 but reported 11 AI crawlers not explicitly managed, including GPTBot, ClaudeBot, PerplexityBot, Google-Extended, Applebot-Extended, Bytespider, and CCBot.
impact: The site has no explicit policy for AI crawler access, training, and citation behavior.
fix: Add explicit allow/disallow groups for target AI crawlers in robots.txt based on the business policy.

evidence: llms_txt_checker.py found /llms.txt HTTP 200 with quality score 55/100, no title, no description, and no llms-full.txt.
impact: AI answer engines get weaker site context and less reliable citation guidance.
fix: Start llms.txt with a title, add a blockquote description, curate priority links, and consider adding llms-full.txt.

finding: Several images lack useful alt text and many lack explicit dimensions.
evidence: parse_html.py found 21 images; local count found 4 missing/empty alt attributes and 17 missing width/height attributes.
impact: Missing alt weakens accessibility and image search context; missing dimensions can contribute to layout shifts.
fix: Add descriptive alt text to non-decorative images and explicit width/height or CSS aspect-ratio for all layout images.

finding: Two destination pages have weak internal link support and multiple links have no anchor text.
evidence: internal_links.py crawled 19 pages and found potential orphan pages /viajes-a-medida-lanzarote and /viajes-a-medida-italia with one incoming link each; 28 links have no anchor text.
impact: Low internal link equity and empty anchors reduce crawl context and topical relevance signals.
fix: Add contextual links to Lanzarote and Italia from destination/category/blog pages and ensure image/card links have accessible text or aria-labels.

finding: Homepage text is harder to read than ideal for a consumer travel landing page.
evidence: readability.py measured Flesch reading ease 21.6, grade 14.4, 27.4% complex words, and average paragraph length of 71 sentences.
impact: Difficult copy can reduce engagement, comprehension, and conversion on mobile.
fix: Break long review/copy blocks into shorter paragraphs, simplify sentence structure, and keep core commercial sections scan-friendly.

finding: Heading text extraction shows concatenated words in H1/H2 content.
evidence: parse_html.py extracted H1 as 'Viajes a medidapara quienesbuscan algo más' and H2 examples such as '¿Por quésomosmejores?' and 'Viajes personalizadosdiseñados contigo'.
impact: If the DOM text lacks spaces around styled spans, screen readers and search parsers may receive less natural heading text.
fix: Add literal whitespace around inline heading spans or adjust markup so extracted text reads naturally.

finding: Core homepage metadata is present and well aligned.
evidence: parse_html.py found title 'Viajes a medida y experiencias auténticas | Viajes UKIYO', meta description, index/follow robots, and canonical https://viajesukiyo.com/.
impact: Search engines have clear indexability, canonical, and SERP snippet signals.
fix: Maintain unique title and description on every key destination and itinerary page.

finding: Homepage JSON-LD exists and uses useful site-level entities.
evidence: parse_html.py found JSON-LD graph with TravelAgency/Organization, WebSite SearchAction, ImageObject, and WebPage; validate_schema.py exited successfully.
impact: Structured data supports entity understanding and brand/site context.
fix: Enhance the TravelAgency entity with contactPoint, address/areaServed, and sameAs profiles where accurate.
